sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: HGER ETF Holdings List — NYSE:HGER
feed.summary: This article provides the holdings list for the Harbor All-Weather Inflation Focus ETF (HGER) as of April 14, 2026. The ETF's portfolio is primarily composed of various United States Treasury Bills with different maturity dates, as well as a significant portion in general equity and U.S. Dollar holdings. The largest individual holdings include Treasury Bills maturing in June and September 2026.

feed.title: Beeline, a Bain-backed biotech, debuts with immune drugs from Bristol Myers
feed.summary: Beeline Medicines has launched with $300 million in funding from Bain Capital and a pipeline of five experimental immune drugs licensed from Bristol Myers Squibb. The company's lead candidate, afimetoran, is an oral treatment for lupus currently in Phase 2 trials, with plans for a pivotal development program soon. Beeline aims to develop "precision therapies" for various immune diseases under the leadership of former SpringWorks Therapeutics CEO Saqib Islam.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Digital Barrier Notes linked to the lesser performing of the Russell 2000® Index and the S&P 500® Index, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes provide a fixed return of at least 9.25% at maturity if both indices are at or above 65% of their initial value. Investors face the risk of losing some or all of their principal if either index falls below this barrier amount.

feed.title: Umicore - Transparency notifications by BlackRock, Inc.
feed.summary: Umicore received transparency notifications from BlackRock, Inc. regarding changes in its direct voting rights. BlackRock, Inc. initially crossed downwards through the 5% legal threshold on April 8, 2026, and then crossed upwards through the same threshold on April 9, 2026. As of April 9, 2026, BlackRock, Inc.'s total holding of direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments in Umicore stands at 5.69%.

feed.title: What John Deere’s $99M settlement means for Wisconsin farmers
feed.summary: John Deere has agreed to a $99 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit, meaning Wisconsin farmers who paid for repairs to large agricultural equipment since January 2018 are eligible for refunds. Additionally, all farmers with John Deere equipment will gain access to parts and tools for repairs for the next 10 years, addressing "right-to-repair" concerns. The settlement resolves accusations of monopolizing equipment maintenance by limiting access to repair software, a significant issue for farmers facing equipment downtime.

feed.title: Allegion pitches tools to cut data center build errors and delays
feed.summary: Allegion is set to exhibit its mission-critical data center solutions at Data Center World in Washington, D.C., from April 20-23, 2026. The company will showcase an integrated portfolio, including specialty doors, electronic latch retraction systems, and heavy-duty closers, alongside its Overtur design platform. These tools aim to streamline installations, minimize errors, and accelerate data center deployment, addressing critical security and operational challenges in the rapidly expanding data center market.

feed.title: (Energie): AES Corp's Data Center Pivot Could Drive Renewable Surge
feed.summary: AES Corp is strategically positioning itself to capitalize on the booming AI data center market by securing massive renewable energy deals. The company's focus on an integrated model, combining generation with storage and grid services, gives it an edge over competitors like NextEra Energy. By securing long-term power purchase agreements, AES is diversifying its revenue and mitigating risks, making it an attractive option for investors seeking stable growth in the energy sector.

feed.title: Snap-on Inc stock (US8335511049): Why its industrial tool dominance matters more now for investors
feed.summary: Snap-on Inc (US8335511049) is highlighted as a resilient investment due to its focus on professional tools, direct sales model, and strong financials. The company's segments, including Tools Group, Commercial & Electrical, and Repair Systems, position it well in various industrial and automotive sectors. Snap-on's consistent dividend growth, share repurchases, and strategic investments underline its appeal for long-term investors seeking stability and value.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) Price Target Cut to $28.00 by Analysts at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has cut its price target for Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) from $29.00 to $28.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential upside of approximately 15%. This comes despite Weyerhaeuser beating EPS estimates with ($0.09) for the quarter, though revenue of $1.54 billion missed expectations and was down 9.8% year-over-year. The article also notes significant insider purchases and high institutional ownership, indicating confidence in the stock.

feed.title: Teradyne Stock Is Up 400% and Analysts Are Still Calling for More Upside in 2026
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) stock has surged over 400% from its 52-week low, driven by strong AI-related chip demand, positioning it as a key AI infrastructure play. The company reported impressive Q4 2025 results and issued an upbeat Q1 2026 guidance, significantly exceeding analyst estimates. While some analysts maintain lower price targets, TIKR's valuation model suggests significant upside, with a mid-case target of $612, supported by robust revenue growth and expanding net income margins fueled by the continued AI buildout.

feed.title: Roofr Partners with Verisk for Enhanced Claims Management (VRSK)
feed.summary: Roofr has partnered with Verisk to integrate Verisk's Xactimate solution for enhanced property claims management, aiming to improve estimate accuracy, workflow efficiency, and claims resolution. Verisk (VRSK) has a P/E ratio of 27.15 and a GF Score of 82/100, indicating strong potential for long-term returns despite recent insider selling activity. This collaboration is expected to streamline the claims process for contractors by enabling direct uploads into Xactimate, eliminating manual redrawing.

feed.title: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) Price Target Lowered to $380.00 at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has lowered its price target for Stryker (NYSE:SYK) to $380.00 from $395.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, despite a consensus of "Moderate Buy" among analysts. The medical technology company recently beat quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, but a director, Ronda E. Stryker, sold a significant block of 250,000 shares for over $90 million. Institutional investors, however, have been increasing their stakes in the company.

feed.title: Monarch Tractor Closes, Sells Assets to Caterpillar
feed.summary: Monarch Tractor, an autonomous electric tractor company founded in 2018, recently closed its doors and sold its assets to Caterpillar. Despite raising over $240 million in venture capital, Monarch struggled to commercialize its technology, acknowledging unforeseen challenges in scaling a new tractor platform. The company is now pivoting to a technology licensing model for broader applications in both agricultural and construction machinery.

feed.title: AEP Maintained by Barclays -- Price Target Raised to $135
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained an Equal-Weight rating on American Electric Power (AEP) and increased its price target from $128 to $135. Despite this, GuruFocus assesses AEP as 29.1% overvalued with a GF Value™ of $104.33, though it notes strong fundamentals with a GF Score™ of 80/100, primarily driven by profitability and momentum. Insider selling activity further suggests potential caution regarding the stock's current valuation.

feed.title: As Food Giant Sysco Tightens Its Hold, We Asked Local Chefs and Restaurateurs How Much to Worry
feed.summary: Food giant Sysco's proposed $29.1 billion acquisition of Restaurant Depot is causing significant anxiety among independent restaurant owners and chefs. Many rely on Restaurant Depot as an affordable cash-and-carry warehouse for supplies, offering a crucial alternative to larger distributors like Sysco. Industry experts and restaurateurs fear the merger will further consolidate the food distribution market, leading to fewer options and higher prices for independent businesses and ultimately for consumers.

feed.title: Deere & Co Stock (DE) Moved Down by 3.42% on Apr 15: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Deere & Co's (DE) stock declined by 3.42% on April 15, driven primarily by weaker agricultural equipment sales in the U.S. and Canada and large institutional investors reducing their holdings in Q4 2025. The company's recent $99 million settlement in "right to repair" litigation also contributed to investor caution, overshadowing earlier positive earnings and some analyst upgrades. Despite some positive technical indicators, the negative catalysts related to core agricultural demand and institutional capital flow appear to be currently dominating the stock's performance.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble vs. Colgate: Which Dividend Stock Rules Them All
feed.summary: This article compares Procter & Gamble (PG) and Colgate-Palmolive (CL), two prominent dividend stocks in the consumer staples sector, highlighting their recent financial performance and strategic initiatives. While P&G reported flat organic sales and faces restructuring costs and tariffs, Colgate showed stronger organic growth but suffered a goodwill impairment. The article concludes that Colgate offers near-term momentum, but P&G's scale and longer dividend history make it a durable option for long-term income investors, especially given its current valuation.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc Stock (CAT) Moved Down by 3.49% on Apr 15: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) shares declined by 3.49% on April 15th due to renewed investor concerns over valuation, impending earnings, and skepticism about its role in the AI infrastructure boom. Macroeconomic uncertainties, including slowing global growth and softness in construction spending, further contributed to the downward pressure. Despite recent positive analyst ratings, these broader headwinds and valuation worries overshadowed a confident outlook.

feed.title: Ball Corp. stock (US05722G1004): Is sustainable packaging its edge in a green economy?
feed.summary: Ball Corp. (US05722G1004) is positioned as a leader in aluminum packaging, benefiting from the global shift towards sustainable alternatives to plastic. Its focus on recyclable aluminum beverage cans and efficient production provides a competitive edge, driving demand and stable cash flows for investors. Analysts view the company favorably due to consistent execution, margin expansion potential, and long-term tailwinds from recycling trends, making it a resilient industrial play for diversified portfolios.

feed.title: Regions Bank shares financial tips for Arkansas students
feed.summary: Regions Bank manager Bridget Aaron appeared on Arkansas Today to offer financial advice to high school and college students in Arkansas. The tips are aimed at helping students as they prepare for graduation and manage their finances. This initiative underscores the bank's commitment to financial education for young adults.

feed.title: Xcel Energy expands solar and battery storage to enhance cost management and electric reliability
feed.summary: Xcel Energy has brought new solar generation and battery energy storage systems online at its Plant X site in Texas and Cunningham Generating Station in New Mexico. These projects aim to enhance cost management and electric reliability by utilizing fuel-free solar power and storing energy during periods of lower cost for use during high demand. The company is also proposing additional battery storage systems to further support grid stability and integrate more renewable energy.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Capped Notes linked to the least performing of the Dow Jones Industrial Average®, the Nasdaq-100 Index®, and the S&P 500® Index, with maturity due November 1, 2027. These notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co., designed for investors seeking exposure to appreciation up to a maximum return of at least 21.85% at maturity, while accepting repayment of at least 95.00% of their principal. The offering highlights various risk considerations including potential principal loss, credit risks of the issuer and guarantor, and market factors, and details tax treatment as contingent payment debt instruments.

feed.title: QUALCOMM Incorporated stock (US7475251036): Is its AI chip edge strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: QUALCOMM's dual-engine business model, with high-margin chip licensing and semiconductor sales, positions it well in the growing AI and 5G markets. Its Snapdragon platforms are integral to smartphones, automotive, and IoT, driving revenue growth despite competition and regulatory scrutiny. The company's U.S.-centric R&D and strong intellectual property make it an appealing investment for diversification and exposure to tech innovation.

feed.title: Greene County hospital serves Anthem with breach notice over underpayments
feed.summary: A hospital in Greene County has issued a breach of contract notice to Anthem over alleged systematic underpayments for patient care. The hospital claims Anthem has been underpaying for services dating back to 2017, amounting to millions of dollars. The notice aims to push Anthem into arbitration to resolve the dispute, highlighting growing tensions between healthcare providers and large insurers over reimbursement rates.

feed.title: Many companies want clean energy. Georgia Power will soon let them build their own
feed.summary: Georgia Power is launching a new program called the Customer-Identified Resource program, allowing customers to propose and fund their own clean energy projects within the state. This initiative aims to help companies with emissions-reduction targets meet their goals and could accelerate clean energy development, potentially reducing the need for natural gas. The program received bipartisan support and is expected to open by summer, offering a new mechanism for Georgia Power customers to integrate renewable energy into the grid.

feed.title: Adimab Collaborator GSK Receives Additional Marketing Approval for Depemokimab
feed.summary: Adimab announced that its collaborator, GSK plc, has received additional marketing approvals for depemokimab in Europe and China, adding to prior approvals in the US, Japan, and UK. Depemokimab is the first ultra-long-acting biologic of its kind and was developed using Adimab's proprietary yeast-based technology, which GSK licensed to enable in-house antibody discovery and optimization. This marks the sixth Adimab-associated product to reach market, validating Adimab's Platform Transfer business model.

feed.title: T-Mobile Arena adds Tito’s lounge and annual charity program
feed.summary: T-Mobile Arena and Tito's Handmade Vodka have announced a multi-year sponsorship agreement, making Tito's an official venue sponsor. The partnership includes the creation of "The 1997 Lounge" at the arena, offering special brand activations and custom cocktails. A key component of this collaboration is a commitment to community, with both organizations pledging to launch initiatives including an annual donation to a joint charity.

feed.title: Delta quietly removes its net-zero 2030 goal as airlines worldwide abandon climate promises
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has quietly removed its 2030 goal to use 10% sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and rephrased its 2050 net-zero emissions target as an "aspiration," citing slow progress in SAF technology. While Delta remains committed to cleaner fuels, its 2025 SAF usage was only about 0.5% of its total jet fuel, highlighting industry-wide struggles to meet climate objectives. Other airlines like Southwest and Air New Zealand have also scaled back or dropped their ambitious carbon-reduction goals.

feed.title: Raymond James Promotes Firm Veteran to COO of Private Client Group
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has promoted Patrick O’Connor, a two-decade firm veteran, to Chief Operating Officer for its Private Client Group. Effective May 15, O'Connor will oversee growth initiatives, strategic execution, and advisor-facing support across the firm's various platforms. He will report to Tash Elwyn, president of the Private Client Group, and support advisors in deepening client relationships.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share at $413
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC recently purchased a single share of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL) for $413.96, increasing its direct ownership to 3,435,496 shares. This transaction occurred amidst a turbulent period for TPL, which saw a 16% decline in shares following the death of board member Murray Stahl, who was also CEO of Horizon Kinetics. Despite the stock being deemed overvalued by InvestingPro, KeyBanc reiterated an Overweight rating and raised its price target to $639, citing opportunities in power generation and data centers.

feed.title: Prologis Q1 2026 Preview: Leveraging Strengths Amid Market Woes
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) is set to release its Q1 2026 earnings, with analysts expecting a 5.8% year-over-year revenue increase and a 4.23% FFO per share increase. The company is benefiting from a balanced industrial real estate market where demand for modern facilities remains strong, especially in inland markets. Despite market challenges, Prologis is in a strong financial position, leveraging its global presence and acquisition strategy, with management anticipating occupancy to rebuild throughout the year after a seasonal dip.

feed.title: Can AI and Forecasting Tools Boost Darden's Restaurant Efficiency?
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants, Inc. is leveraging AI and forecasting tools to enhance restaurant efficiency, focusing on improved demand forecasting, streamlined operations, and corporate-level task automation. While not replacing employees, this technology aids in better staffing, inventory management, and cost control. Competitors like McDonald's and Yum! Brands are also heavily investing in similar AI-driven solutions to optimize their global operations.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Welcomes Wyndham to Rewards Points Transfer Program
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has announced that Wyndham Rewards has joined its Rewards Points Transfer program, allowing Wells Fargo credit cardholders to transfer points at a 1:2 ratio. This partnership aims to provide greater flexibility and value for customers, enabling them to convert everyday spending into travel experiences by accessing Wyndham's global portfolio of hotels and various redemption options. This new feature is available for all Wells Fargo credit cards earning rewards points, including the Autograph and Autograph Journey cards.

feed.title: Abbott seen posting modest Q1 earnings amid Nutrition segment slowdown
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories is projected to announce modest top and bottom-line growth for its first quarter on April 16. EPS is expected to increase by 5.5% to $1.15, with revenue also showing a slight rise, despite recent challenges in the company's Nutrition segment. Analyst revisions for both EPS and revenue have primarily been downward, indicating limited market optimism.

feed.title: Moderna to Report Q1 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Moderna, Inc. announced it will host a live conference call and webcast on Friday, May 1, 2026, to report its first-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a corporate update. This event is highly anticipated by investors and the healthcare industry, as Moderna's progress in mRNA technology has significant implications for future medicine. The webcast will be available on Moderna's website for one year following the call.

feed.title: KLA shares fall as U.S. export-control fallout weighs on chip-equipment group
feed.summary: KLA (KLAC) shares dropped by 4.4% due to renewed concerns over tighter U.S. export controls on semiconductor equipment and services to China. This decline was triggered by a peer's disclosure of an expected revenue headwind from a BIS export-control update, leading to a broader selloff in the sector. Insider trading data shows recent sales by KLA executives, while institutional holdings data indicates both significant additions and reductions by various firms.

feed.title: Corning shares fall 3.2% amid profit-taking and fresh filing-related overhang
feed.summary: Corning Incorporated (GLW) shares fell 3.2% due to factors including near-term profit-taking after a recent rally and investors digesting new annual report and proxy materials. The decline was also potentially influenced by incremental share supply from equity awards vesting around mid-April and a broader sector rotation, as market commentaries had previously noted a re-rating tied to AI-related optical connectivity demand. Insider trading data shows significant sales by company executives over the past six months, with no purchases.

feed.title: Liquefied natural gas: How do LNG-powered cruise ships work?
feed.summary: This article explains how LNG-powered cruise ships operate and their environmental benefits, such as reducing greenhouse gas and sulfur emissions. It highlights the growing number of LNG vessels in the cruise industry, naming key operators like Carnival Corporation and Royal Caribbean Group, and lists current and upcoming LNG-powered ships. Additionally, it touches on other sustainable technologies being explored, including flex-fuel engines and hydrogen power.

feed.title: Nvidia GPU warranty claims cost the company nearly $1BN in 2025
feed.summary: Nvidia's GPU warranty claims cost increased by over 1,000% to $894 million in 2025, significantly higher than the previous year's $81 million. This surge, detailed in a Warranty Week report, is attributed to rising VRAM prices and an increase in the warranty rate relative to revenue, from 0.1% in 2024 to 0.9% in 2025. While AMD also saw an increase in warranty costs, their rise was less dramatic.

feed.title: Petrobras & Honeywell Launch Brazil's First Large-Scale Ethanol-to-Jet Fuel Project - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Petrobras has selected Honeywell UOP's Ethanol-to-Jet (ETJ) technology for Brazil's first large-scale Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) project at its REPLAN refinery in Sao Paulo. This initiative aims to produce up to 10,000 barrels per day of SAF using ethanol as a feedstock, reinforcing Brazil's commitment to sustainable energy and leadership in SAF production. The project is expected to significantly reduce aviation carbon emissions and meet growing SAF demand.

feed.title: Raymond James reiterates HubSpot stock rating on product innovation By Investing.com
feed.summary: Raymond James has reaffirmed its Outperform rating and $280 price target for HubSpot Inc (NYSE:HUBS), citing the company's recent platform and product enhancements, including a new AEO offering and AI-driven customer engagement features. Despite a 62% share price drop over the past year, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued. The firm believes HubSpot's AI strategy positions it as a trusted partner, benefiting platform app vendors over time.

feed.title: Hawkins Inc stock (US4202611099): Why water treatment chemicals now drive steady investor returns?
feed.summary: Hawkins Inc (US4202611099) offers stable investor returns primarily through its focus on water treatment chemicals, a sector with essential, recession-resistant demand. The company's business model emphasizes value-added distribution and regional strength, positioning it well to benefit from U.S. infrastructure spending and an increasing need for cleaner water. Analysts view Hawkins favorably due to its consistent execution, margin discipline, and low debt, making it a reliable pick for balanced portfolios.

feed.title: H2O America (NASDAQ:HTO) Given New $60.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has reduced its price target for H2O America (NASDAQ:HTO) from $63.00 to $60.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating, suggesting a potential upside of nearly 5%. Despite mixed analyst sentiment, the consensus rating for HTO is a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $61.40. Notably, major shareholder Atlas Infrastructure Partners recently increased its stake by acquiring over 50,000 shares for close to $3 million, indicating insider confidence in the utilities provider.

feed.title: Assessing Hesai Group (HSAI) Valuation After MODEX 2026 Robotics Partnership With Thoro.ai
feed.summary: Hesai Group (HSAI) is gaining attention after showcasing its JT128 3D lidar at MODEX 2026, integrated into Thoro.ai's robotics platform. Despite a recent share price decline year-to-date, the company has shown strong longer-term returns and impressive financial performance in 2025 with significant revenue growth and GAAP net income. While a user narrative suggests Hesai is undervalued, another perspective highlights its high P/E ratio compared to industry averages, indicating potential valuation risk.

feed.title: Honda, Toyota form assembly-oriented lobbying group in Canada as trade pressures build
feed.summary: Honda and Toyota have formed a new lobbying group in Canada focused on assembly, in response to growing trade pressures. This move follows the disbanding of the Japanese Automobile Manufacturers Association of Canada (JAMA) in 2021, which previously represented their interests. The new group aims to specifically address the unique needs of manufacturing operations for these two major automakers in the Canadian market.

feed.title: Month-end portfolio data now available for Federated Hermes Premier Municipal Income Fund
feed.summary: Federated Hermes, Inc. announced that the monthly fund composition and performance data for its Premier Municipal Income Fund (NYSE: FMN) as of March 30, 2026, is now available. This data can be accessed on FederatedHermes.com/us, and hard copies can be requested via phone or email. Federated Hermes, a global leader in active investing, reported $902.6 billion in assets under management as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ:GT) Price Target Cut to $9.00 by Analysts at TD Cowen
feed.summary: TD Cowen analysts have reduced their price target for Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ:GT) from $10.00 to $9.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, implying a potential 32.5% upside. This adjustment follows Goodyear's recent quarterly earnings report where they missed consensus EPS estimates but exceeded revenue expectations. The stock currently holds a "Hold" consensus rating from MarketBeat, with an average price target of $10.11.

feed.title: Caterpillar Turns To Self-Driving Tractors: What Buying The 'Tesla Of Agriculture' Could Mean
feed.summary: Caterpillar is reportedly acquiring Monarch Tractor, a self-driving electric tractor company, signaling a major move into agricultural technology. This acquisition follows Caterpillar's strong performance and an expanded partnership with NVIDIA, aiming to integrate AI and autonomous capabilities across its equipment. The move positions Caterpillar to compete with rivals like Deere & Co in the evolving market for AI-powered and self-driving agricultural machinery.

feed.title: AMD stock price forecast: $252.16–$258.81 range as AMD trades flat
feed.summary: AMD is currently trading at $256.44, up 0.54% on the day, showing strong bullish momentum above its key moving averages. The company recently invested $60 million in autonomous driving startup Wayve and reported robust Q4 2025 revenue and significant data center growth. Analysts forecast AMD to consolidate within a $252.16–$258.81 range this week, while acknowledging overbought conditions, and emphasize monitoring $258.81 as a critical breakout level for further upside.

feed.title: Shareholders Sue Real Estate Group FBRT Over Dodgy Dividend Claims
feed.summary: Franklin BSP Realty Trust (FBRT) is facing a class action lawsuit filed on February 26, 2026, alleging that the company misled investors by making false claims about its ability to maintain dividends. Shareholders claim they purchased FBRT stock at artificially inflated prices between November 5, 2024, and February 11, 2026, due to the company's and its senior officers' omissions of truthful information regarding its dividend sustainability. The lawsuit comes after the company announced a significant reduction in its quarterly dividend, admitting previous undercovering of dividends, which allegedly caused financial losses for investors.
